verbatim_text: And finally in the third layer you can compute S3 from S2 and I3. So each layer successively computes a single state. So you can get a kind of diagonal algorithm that looks something like this. In the paper we also had something called the parallel algorithm. You can read more about it if you want in the paper. But it turns out for certain easy variants of the problem you can compute the state entirely in a fixed number of layers.
